lesson.title: Lesson 91: Three Men in a Basket

rights.notes: Migrated from legacy published content; migration does not establish redistribution rights.

segments.text: First listen and then answer the question.

translation.text: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Where was the station's commanding officer?
A pilot noticed a balloon which seemed to be making for a Royal Air Force station nearby.

translation.text: 一名飞行员发现一只气球似乎正飞向附近的皇家空军基地。

segments.text: He informed the station at once, but no one there was able to explain the mystery.

translation.text: 他立即通知基地，但那里没人能解释这个谜团。

segments.text: The officer in the control tower was very angry when he heard the news because balloons can be a great danger to aircraft.

translation.text: 控制塔里的军官听到消息后非常生气，因为气球可能给飞机带来巨大危险。

segments.text: He said that someone might be spying on the station and the pilot was ordered to keep track of the strange object.

translation.text: 他说或许有人在侦察基地，飞行员随即奉命跟踪这个奇怪的物体。

segments.text: The pilot managed to circle the balloon for some time.

translation.text: 飞行员设法绕着气球飞了一段时间。

segments.text: He could make out three men in a basket under it and one of them was holding a pair of binoculars.

translation.text: 他能辨认出气球下面的篮子里有三个人，其中一人拿着一副望远镜。

segments.text: When the balloon was over the station, the pilot saw one of the men taking photographs.

translation.text: 气球飞越基地时，飞行员看见其中一人在拍照。

segments.text: Soon afterwards, the balloon began to descend and it landed near an airfield.

translation.text: 不久之后，气球开始下降，降落在一座机场附近。

segments.text: The police were called in, but they could not arrest anyone, for the basket contained two members of Parliament and the commanding officer of the station.

translation.text: 警察被叫来，却无法逮捕任何人，因为篮子里坐着两名议员和这座基地的指挥官。

segments.text: As the commanding officer explained later, one half of the station did not know what the other half was doing.

translation.text: 正如指挥官后来解释的那样，基地的一半人不知道另一半人在做什么。
